name: Upside Down Apple Tart with Orange and Oatmeal Pastry
recipeIngredient: cardamom seeds from 4-5 pods, finely ground
recipeInstructions:
1. TO MAKE THE PASTRY: Place the flour, oatmeal, sugar, and salt in a food processor and process once to mix. Add butter cut into small pieces, then process briefly until mixture resembles rough breadcrumbs. Incorporate orange zest; add enough orange juice for dough to stick together. Form into a ball, cover, and refrigerate while preparing apples.
2. Prepare the 7-1/2 to 8-inch cake pan (do not use one with a loose base). Melt butter over the base and sides.
3. Peel apples and cut each in half, removing cores. Cut thinly into half-moons. Toss slices immediately with lemon juice to prevent discoloration.
4. Combine marmalade, honey, and ground cardamom seeds. Spread mixture on buttered pan bottom. Arrange apple slices neatly, overlapping, in the pan.
5. Remove pastry from refrigerator. Roll lightly on a floured board larger than the pan using a well-floured rolling pin. Carefully roll back over the pin and place it over the apples. If pastry tears, press edges together. Press overlapping edge into sides of the pan and pierce two holes for steam release.
6. BAKE: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Cook tart in center for 25 minutes, then reduce temperature to 325°F (165°C) and cook for another 30 minutes. Turn off oven, leave door slightly ajar, and let tart rest 10-15 minutes before serving.
7. SERVING: Slide tart from pan onto a flat plate, upside-down. Serve warm with crème fraîche, cream, or yogurt as desired.
Note: This recipe can be made in advance and reheated. Quinces can substitute for apples if available.
